title: lakefetch - Development Status

title: lakefetch - Development Status
text: lakefetch calculates fetch (open water distance) wave exposure metrics freshwater lake sampling sites. downloads lake boundaries OpenStreetMap, calculates directional fetch using ray-casting, provides wave exposure classifications. GitHub: https://github.com/jeremylfarrell/lakefetch

title: lakefetch - Development Status
text: checks pass locally (0 errors, 0 warnings, 1 note). Waiting feedback Lucas Fenly re-running win-builder/R-hub submitting CRAN.

title: lakefetch - Development Status
text: Custom column names (R/data_loading.R): load_sites() now accepts lat_col, lon_col, site_col, lake_col arguments explicitly specify column names. Useful datasets like GLEON Info_Data.csv lake name column just “name”. Progress bars (R/fetch_core.R): Added txtProgressBar fetch calculation site buffering loops. shown interactive sessions. new dependencies (uses base R utils). Shiny app performance (R/shiny_app.R): Rose plots ray geometries now generated demand (marker click) instead pre-rendered sites startup. Marker clustering enabled >30 sites. fixes crash using fetch_app() large datasets like GLEON 433-site dataset. Invalid coordinate filtering (R/data_loading.R): Added (0,0) coordinate detection removal warning. Optimized OSM downloads (R/lake_sources.R): Refactored download_lake_osm() geographically spread datasets like GLEON (429 sites worldwide). site spread exceeds 0.5°, sites grouped spatial clusters (grid-based 0.1° resolution via cluster_sites()) cluster gets small bbox (~0.1°) single natural=water Overpass query. replaces old single-bbox approach cover entire globe timeout. Key features: server rotation across 3 Overpass endpoints, 1s rate limiting queries, automatic retry server errors, minimum area filter (0.0001 km²) remove tiny water bodies. Helper functions extracted top-level: extract_osm_polys(), query_osm_by_name(), download_lake_osm_single(). small-spread sites (≤0.5°), original single-bbox behavior preserved.

title: lakefetch - Development Status
text: Fixed name-based lake matching (R/lake_sources.R, Pass 3 assign_sites_to_lakes()): Added distance check (tolerance_m * 5, default 500m) sites aren’t silently assigned -named lake far away. Sites fail proximity check skipped warning. fixes Raquette Lake mismatch bug reported colleague testing Adirondack sites. Clarified Shiny app instructions (R/shiny_app.R, line 496): Changed “Click lake” “Click lake polygon analyze new point lake.” per Kait’s feedback original wording implied lake map clicked.

text: Users can run app without R coding - just upload CSV: launches browser-based app users can: - Upload CSV lat/lon columns - Automatically download lake boundaries - View interactive map fetch rays - Download results CSV GeoPackage
code: library(lakefetch) fetch_app_upload()

title: lakefetch - Development Status
text: Automatic lake boundary download OpenStreetMap Multi-lake batch processing - handles CSV sites multiple lakes Directional fetch - ray-casting configurable angle resolution (default 5°) "top3" - mean 3 highest directional fetches (default) "max" - maximum directional fetch "cosine" - SPM/CERC cosine-weighted method Wave metrics - orbital velocity using SMB equations depth attenuation Exposure classification - Sheltered/Moderate/Exposed (configurable thresholds) Depth estimation - empirical estimation lake surface area (Cael et al. 2017) Optional NHD integration - outlet/inlet detection US lakes Optional weather integration - historical wind data Open-Meteo API Interactive visualization - Shiny app Leaflet maps

title: lakefetch - Development Status
code: # View current options lakefetch_options()  # Change options lakefetch_options(   fetch_method = "cosine",           # SPM method   exposure_sheltered_m = 2000,       # Custom threshold   exposure_exposed_m = 6000,         # Custom threshold   default_depth_m = 5,               # For orbital velocity   angle_resolution_deg = 10          # Faster but less precise )  # Reset to defaults lakefetch_reset_options()

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
text: lakefetch package calculates fetch (open water distance) wave exposure metrics lake sampling points. Fetch important physical parameter influences wave energy, sediment resuspension, habitat characteristics.

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
code: install.packages("lakefetch")  # Optional packages for extra features: # install.packages("nhdplusTools")                      # NHD integration # install.packages(c("shiny", "leaflet", "base64enc"))  # Interactive app

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
text: CSV columns site names coordinates. Column names starting “lat” “lon” automatically detected.
code: library(lakefetch)  # From CSV file sites <- load_sites("my_lake_sites.csv")  # Or create manually sites <- data.frame(   Site = c("Site_A", "Site_B", "Site_C"),   latitude = c(43.42, 43.43, 43.41),   longitude = c(-73.69, -73.68, -73.70) ) sites <- load_sites(sites)

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
code: # Automatically download from OpenStreetMap lake <- get_lake_boundary(sites)  # Or load from a local shapefile lake <- get_lake_boundary(sites, file = "lake_boundary.shp")

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
code: results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake)  # View results head(results$results)

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
code: # Map of sites plot_fetch_map(results)  # Bar chart plot_fetch_bars(results)  # Interactive app fetch_app(results)

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
text: fetch_calculate() function returns list containing: fetch_0, fetch_5, …, fetch_355: Fetch distance direction (meters) fetch_mean: Average fetch across directions fetch_max: Maximum fetch (longest open water distance) fetch_effective: Mean three longest fetch values orbital_effective: Estimated wave orbital velocity (m/s) exposure_category: Classification “Sheltered”, “Moderate”, “Exposed” lakes: lake polygon(s) used calculation angles: angles (degrees) used ray-casting

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
text: Sites classified based effective fetch:

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
text: Customize calculation parameters:
code: # View current options lakefetch_options()  # Change options lakefetch_options(   buffer_distance_m = 20,      # GPS accuracy buffer   angle_resolution_deg = 10,   # Direction resolution (degrees)   max_fetch_m = 30000,         # Maximum fetch distance   use_parallel = TRUE          # Parallel processing for multiple lakes )  # Reset to defaults lakefetch_reset_options()

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
text: nhdplusTools package installed, lakefetch can add hydrological context National Hydrography Dataset: - Lake outlet inlet locations - Distance direction outlet/inlets site - Connectivity classification (Headwater, Drainage, Terminal, Isolated) - Outlet stream order - Watershed area
code: # NHD context is added automatically if available results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake, add_context = TRUE)  # Check connectivity table(results$results$connectivity_class)

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
text: sites span multiple lakes, lakefetch handles automatically:
code: # Sites are assigned to their containing lake polygon sites_with_lakes <- assign_sites_to_lakes(lake$sites, lake$all_lakes)  # Check assignment table(sites_with_lakes$lake_name)

title: Getting Started with lakefetch
code: # To CSV (without geometry) write.csv(sf::st_drop_geometry(results$results),           "fetch_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)  # To GeoPackage (with geometry) sf::st_write(results$results, "fetch_results.gpkg")  # Export ray geometries for GIS rays <- create_ray_geometries(results) sf::st_write(rays, "fetch_rays.gpkg")

title: Validation of lakefetch Calculations
text: Shore Protection Manual defines effective fetch using radial measurements weighted direction. lakefetch uses similar ray-casting approach configurable angular resolution (default 5°). ### Mason et al. (2018) Mason et al. used comparable ray-casting approach create effective fetch maps Great Lakes 30m resolution. methodology validated USACE Wave Information Studies (R² = 0.635).

title: Validation of lakefetch Calculations
text: lakefetch calculates effective fetch mean top 3 directional fetch values. provides robust exposure metric independent specific wind direction, suitable ecological applications wind patterns may vary.

what: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
title: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
text: Calculate fetch (open water distance) wave exposure metrics freshwater lake sampling sites.

title: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
text: Fetch unobstructed distance wind can travel across open water. key physical driver lakes controls wave height, wave energy, shoreline exposure. Fetch influences sediment resuspension, nutrient cycling, littoral habitat structure, distribution aquatic organisms. Despite importance, calculating fetch inland lakes required either manual GIS work tools designed coastal/marine environments handle irregular shorelines small spatial scales typical lakes. lakefetch fills gap providing end--end R workflow freshwater fetch analysis: downloads lake boundary polygons OpenStreetMap, calculates directional fetch via ray-casting algorithm, classifies sites wave exposure, optionally integrates weather data estimate cumulative wave energy. package designed batch processing across many lakes sites, making practical large-scale ecological limnological studies.

title: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
text: lakefetch operates two-dimensional geographic (curvilinear) coordinates. Input data expected latitude/longitude pairs (WGS84, EPSG:4326) sf objects CRS. Internally, spatial calculations performed projected Cartesian coordinate system (UTM zone automatically determined site locations) ensure accurate distance measurements meters. package uses sf package spatial operations compliant PROJ6+ WKT2 coordinate reference system representations.

title: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
text: Install CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("lakefetch") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jeremylfarrell/lakefetch")

title: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
code: library(lakefetch)  # Load sampling sites from a CSV with latitude/longitude columns sites <- load_sites("my_sites.csv")  # Download lake boundaries from OpenStreetMap lake <- get_lake_boundary(sites)  # Calculate fetch for all sites results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake)  # View results - an sf object with fetch values and exposure categories results$results

title: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
text: Load sites CSV data frame containing latitude, longitude, optional site names. Get lake boundaries automatically OpenStreetMap, load shapefiles/geopackages. Calculate fetch casting rays site outward regular angular intervals (default 5 degrees) measuring distance nearest shoreline direction. Classify exposure based effective fetch: Sheltered (< 2.5 km), Moderate (2.5–5 km), Exposed (> 5 km). Thresholds configurable. site, lakefetch returns: Three effective fetch methods available: "top3" (default), "max", "cosine" (SPM/CERC cosine-weighted method Shore Protection Manual).

title: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
text: Automatic boundary download: Downloads lake polygons OpenStreetMap, spatial clustering multi-server fallback large geographically spread datasets. Ray-casting fetch: Measures distance shore configurable angular resolution (default 5 degrees, 72 directions). Multi-lake batch processing: single CSV can contain sites many different lakes; boundaries downloaded fetch calculated lake automatically. Exposure classification: Sites categorized Sheltered, Moderate, Exposed based configurable thresholds. Wave energy estimation: Empirical wave hindcasting using Sverdrup-Munk-Bretschneider (SMB) equations depth-attenuated orbital velocity, based directional fetch historical wind data. Depth estimation: Empirical mean maximum depth lake surface area using global scaling relationship Cael et al. (2017). NHD integration (optional, US lakes): Identifies outlets, inlets, stream order, watershed area, connectivity classification using National Hydrography Dataset via nhdplusTools package. Weather integration (optional): Retrieves historical hourly weather data Open-Meteo API computes windowed summary statistics (wind speed, direction, temperature, precipitation, wave energy) user-specified time periods sampling event. Visualization: Static plots (fetch maps, bar charts, directional rose diagrams) two interactive Shiny applications point--click exploration CSV upload workflows.

title: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
code: library(lakefetch)  # Built-in example data data("adirondack_sites")  # Download lake boundaries from OpenStreetMap lake <- get_lake_boundary(adirondack_sites)  # Calculate fetch with depth estimation results <- fetch_calculate(sites = adirondack_sites, lake = lake)  # Visualize plot_fetch_map(results)      # Map colored by exposure class plot_fetch_bars(results)     # Bar chart of effective fetch plot_fetch_rose(results, 1)  # Rose diagram for the first site  # Interactive Shiny app fetch_app(results)

text: lake boundary shapefile geopackage:
code: lake <- get_lake_boundary(sites, file = "my_lake_boundary.gpkg") results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake)

title: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
text: Small water bodies OpenStreetMap: small lakes ponds (e.g., prairie ponds, stock tanks) may mapped OpenStreetMap. boundary found, get_lake_boundary() error message suggesting supply boundary file via get_lake_boundary(sites, file = "your_boundary.gpkg"). Boundary files can shapefiles, geopackages, format readable sf::st_read(). OpenStreetMap data quality: Lake boundaries OSM vary accuracy completeness region. high-precision studies, consider using authoritative hydrography datasets (e.g., NHD US) boundary sources.

title: Calculate Fetch and Wave Exposure for Lake Sampling Points
text: Several R packages calculate fetch wave exposure, target different use cases: lakefetch distinguished focus freshwater lakes, including: automatic lake boundary download OpenStreetMap spatial clustering large datasets, batch processing sites across multiple lakes, empirical depth estimation, NHD hydrological context US lakes, historical weather/wave energy integration. features designed common ecological workflow analyzing field sampling sites across many lakes.

what: Add Lake Context from NHD — add_lake_context
title: Add Lake Context from NHD — add_lake_context
text: Add hydrological context fetch results using National Hydrography Dataset (NHD). Includes outlet/inlet locations, watershed area, connectivity classification, stream order.

title: Add Lake Context from NHD — add_lake_context
code: add_lake_context(fetch_results, lake_polygons, utm_epsg)

title: Add Lake Context from NHD — add_lake_context
text: fetch_results sf object fetch calculation results lake_polygons sf object lake polygons utm_epsg EPSG code UTM projection

title: Add Lake Context from NHD — add_lake_context
text: sf object additional columns NHD context

title: Add Lake Context from NHD — add_lake_context
text: Requires nhdplusTools package. available, returns input NA columns added consistent output format. Added columns include: nhd_permanent_id: NHD permanent identifier nhd_gnis_name: GNIS name NHD nhd_areasqkm: Area square kilometers NHD outlet_dist_m: Distance outlet meters outlet_bearing: Compass direction outlet inlet_nearest_dist_m: Distance nearest inlet inlet_nearest_bearing: Compass direction nearest inlet inlet_count: Number inlets connectivity_class: Headwater/Drainage/Terminal/Isolated outlet_stream_order: Strahler stream order outlet watershed_area_ha: Watershed area hectares lake_watershed_ratio: Lake area / watershed area

title: Add Lake Context from NHD — add_lake_context
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() csv_path <- system.file("extdata", "sample_sites.csv", package = "lakefetch") sites <- load_sites(csv_path) lake <- get_lake_boundary(sites) results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake) results_with_context <- add_lake_context(results$results, results$lakes, lake$utm_epsg) }

title: Add Depth Information to Fetch Results — add_lake_depth
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() data(adirondack_sites) sites <- load_sites(adirondack_sites) lake <- get_lake_boundary(sites) results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake)  # Add depth estimates results$results <- add_lake_depth(results$results, results$lakes)  # Or provide known depths using an actual lake_osm_id from results lake_id <- results$lakes$osm_id[1] depths <- setNames(15.5, lake_id) results$results <- add_lake_depth(results$results, results$lakes, user_depths = depths) }

code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() csv_path <- system.file("extdata", "sample_sites.csv", package = "lakefetch") sites <- load_sites(csv_path) lake <- get_lake_boundary(sites) results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake)  # Add datetime to results results$results$datetime <- as.POSIXct("2024-07-15 14:00:00")  # Add weather context results_with_weather <- add_weather_context(   results$results,   datetime_col = "datetime" ) }

what: Calculate Fetch for Lake Sampling Sites — fetch_calculate
title: Calculate Fetch for Lake Sampling Sites — fetch_calculate
text: Main entry point fetch calculation. Takes sites lake boundaries, calculates directional fetch using ray-casting, returns results exposure metrics.

title: Calculate Fetch for Lake Sampling Sites — fetch_calculate
code: fetch_calculate(   sites,   lake,   depth_m = NULL,   fetch_method = NULL,   add_context = TRUE,   find_max_fetch = FALSE )

title: Calculate Fetch for Lake Sampling Sites — fetch_calculate
text: sites Data frame sf object site locations lake Lake boundary data get_lake_boundary depth_m Water depth meters orbital velocity calculation. Can single value (applied sites), vector (one per site), NULL use depth sites data default options. fetch_method Method calculating effective fetch. Options: "top3" Mean 3 highest directional fetch values (default) "max" Maximum directional fetch value "cosine" SPM/CERC cosine-weighted average. Uses 9 radials centered direction maximum fetch 6-degree intervals, weighted cosine angle center. Based Shore Protection Manual (1984). NULL, uses value lakefetch_options. add_context Logical; add NHD context available (default TRUE) find_max_fetch Logical; TRUE, finds location lake maximum possible fetch using longest-internal-chord algorithm. result returned $max_fetch element output list. Default FALSE.

title: Calculate Fetch for Lake Sampling Sites — fetch_calculate
text: list elements: results sf object fetch results site lakes sf object lake polygons used angles Vector angles used fetch calculation max_fetch (find_max_fetch = TRUE) sf object one row per lake containing maximum fetch location, chord length (meters), chord bearing (degrees)

title: Calculate Fetch for Lake Sampling Sites — fetch_calculate
text: site, function: Assigns site containing lake polygon Buffers site inward shore (GPS accuracy adjustment) Casts rays directions specified angle resolution Measures distance shore direction Calculates summary metrics (mean, max, effective fetch) Calculates orbital velocity using depth Derives exposure category (Sheltered/Moderate/Exposed) Exposure thresholds can configured via lakefetch_options.

title: Calculate Fetch for Lake Sampling Sites — fetch_calculate
text: Shore Protection Manual (1984). U.S. Army Corps Engineers, Coastal Engineering Research Center. 4th Edition.

title: Calculate Fetch for Lake Sampling Sites — fetch_calculate
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() csv_path <- system.file("extdata", "sample_sites.csv", package = "lakefetch") sites <- load_sites(csv_path) lake <- get_lake_boundary(sites) results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake)  # With explicit depth results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake, depth_m = 5)  # Using cosine-weighted effective fetch (SPM method) results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake, fetch_method = "cosine")  # Access results results$results  # sf with all fetch data results$lakes    # lake polygons  # Find the location with maximum fetch in each lake results <- fetch_calculate(sites, lake, find_max_fetch = TRUE) results$max_fetch  # sf with max fetch location per lake }

title: Get Lake Depth Estimates — get_lake_depth
text: Retrieves estimates lake depth wave calculations. Uses user-provided depth available, otherwise estimates lake surface area using empirical relationships.

title: Get Lake Depth Estimates — get_lake_depth
code: get_lake_depth(   lake_polygon,   site_coords = NULL,   user_depth = NULL,   method = "auto" )

title: Get Lake Depth Estimates — get_lake_depth
text: lake_polygon sf polygon lake site_coords Coordinates sample site (optional, future bathymetry grid support) user_depth User-provided depth meters (highest priority) method Method depth estimation: "auto" "empirical"

title: Get Lake Depth Estimates — get_lake_depth
text: list elements: depth_mean Estimated mean depth meters depth_max Estimated maximum depth meters (available) source Source estimate ("user" "empirical") confidence Confidence level ("high", "medium", "low")

title: Get Lake Depth Estimates — get_lake_depth
text: Depth estimation methods: User-provided: Direct input, highest confidence Empirical: Estimated lake surface area using published relationships empirical method uses relationship Cael et al. (2017): mean_depth ~ 10.3 * area_km2^0.25

title: Get Lake Depth Estimates — get_lake_depth
text: Messager, M.L., Lehner, B., Grill, G., Nedeva, ., Schmitt, O. (2016): Estimating volume age water stored global lakes using geo-statistical approach. Nature Communications, 7: 13603. Cael, B.B., Heathcote, .J., Seekell, D.. (2017): volume mean depth Earth's lakes. Geophysical Research Letters, 44: 209-218.

title: Get Lake Depth Estimates — get_lake_depth
code: data(example_lake)  # With user-provided depth depth <- get_lake_depth(example_lake, user_depth = 8.5)  # Estimate from lake area depth <- get_lake_depth(example_lake) #>   Lake depth estimated from area (3.14 km2): mean ~ 13.7m, max ~ 34.3m

what: Get or Set lakefetch Package Options — lakefetch_options
title: Get or Set lakefetch Package Options — lakefetch_options
text: Get set options control behavior lakefetch functions.

title: Get or Set lakefetch Package Options — lakefetch_options
code: lakefetch_options(...)

title: Get or Set lakefetch Package Options — lakefetch_options
text: ... Named arguments set options. empty, returns current options.

title: Get or Set lakefetch Package Options — lakefetch_options
text: arguments, returns list current options. arguments provided, sets options returns invisible NULL.

title: Get or Set lakefetch Package Options — lakefetch_options
text: Available options: buffer_distance_m GPS accuracy buffer meters (default: 10) angle_resolution_deg Direction resolution degrees (default: 5) max_fetch_m Maximum fetch distance meters (default: 50000) validation_buffer_m Shore detection validation buffer (default: 10) default_wind_speed_ms Default wind speed m/s (default: 10) default_depth_m Default water depth meters (default: 10) gps_tolerance_m Buffer matching sites lakes (default: 100) fetch_method Effective fetch calculation method: "top3" (mean 3 highest directional fetches, default), "max" (maximum directional fetch), "cosine" (SPM/CERC cosine-weighted average across 9 radials 6-degree intervals; see Shore Protection Manual, 1984) exposure_sheltered_m Fetch threshold sites classified "Sheltered" (default: 2500 m). practical default; universal standard exists literature. Adjust based study system. exposure_exposed_m Fetch threshold sites classified "Exposed" (default: 5000 m). Sites thresholds "Moderate". See Mason et al. (2018) Great Lakes exposure mapping methodology. exposure_relative_sheltered Proportion lake maximum fetch sites classified "Sheltered" relative exposure system (default: 0.25). Sites classified relative lake's longest internal chord (maximum possible fetch). exposure_relative_exposed Proportion lake maximum fetch sites classified "Exposed" relative exposure system (default: 0.50). Sites thresholds "Moderate". use_parallel Use parallel processing multi-lake (default: TRUE) use_nhd Use NHD integration available (default: TRUE)

title: Get or Set lakefetch Package Options — lakefetch_options
text: Shore Protection Manual (1984). U.S. Army Corps Engineers, Coastal Engineering Research Center. 4th Edition. Mason, L. ., Riseng, C. M., Laber, . L., & Rutherford, E. S. (2018). Effective fetch relative exposure index maps Laurentian Great Lakes. Scientific Data, 5, 180295.

title: Get or Set lakefetch Package Options — lakefetch_options
code: # Get all options lakefetch_options() #> $buffer_distance_m #> [1] 10 #>  #> $angle_resolution_deg #> [1] 5 #>  #> $max_fetch_m #> [1] 50000 #>  #> $validation_buffer_m #> [1] 10 #>  #> $default_wind_speed_ms #> [1] 10 #>  #> $default_depth_m #> [1] 10 #>  #> $gps_tolerance_m #> [1] 100 #>  #> $fetch_method #> [1] "top3" #>  #> $exposure_sheltered_m #> [1] 2500 #>  #> $exposure_exposed_m #> [1] 5000 #>  #> $exposure_relative_sheltered #> [1] 0.25 #>  #> $exposure_relative_exposed #> [1] 0.5 #>  #> $use_parallel #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $use_nhd #> [1] TRUE #>   # Get specific option lakefetch_options()$buffer_distance_m #> [1] 10  # Set options lakefetch_options(buffer_distance_m = 20, angle_resolution_deg = 10)

code: load_sites(x, lat_col = NULL, lon_col = NULL, site_col = NULL, lake_col = NULL)

title: Load Sites from CSV or Data Frame — load_sites
text: x Either file path CSV file data.frame site data. lat_col Optional character string specifying name latitude column. NULL (default), auto-detects columns starting "lat". lon_col Optional character string specifying name longitude column. NULL (default), auto-detects columns starting "lon". site_col Optional character string specifying name site identifier column. NULL (default), auto-detects column named "site". lake_col Optional character string specifying name lake name column. NULL (default), auto-detects common lake name patterns.

title: Load Sites from CSV or Data Frame — load_sites
text: data.frame columns Site, latitude, longitude, additional columns input. Includes attributes "location_name" "location_column" location detected.

title: Load Sites from CSV or Data Frame — load_sites
text: function: Detects latitude/longitude columns (names starting "lat"/"lon") Cleans coordinate values (removes non-numeric characters) Creates Site column present Removes rows invalid missing coordinates Detects location name data columns filename Column names can specified explicitly using lat_col, lon_col, site_col, lake_col arguments. useful data uses non-standard column names auto-detection find.

title: Load Sites from CSV or Data Frame — load_sites
code: # Load from data frame df <- data.frame(   Site = c("A", "B", "C"),   latitude = c(43.42, 43.43, 43.41),   longitude = c(-73.69, -73.68, -73.70) ) sites <- load_sites(df) #>   Loaded 3 rows with columns: Site, latitude, longitude #>   Using columns: Latitude = latitude, Longitude = longitude #>   Final valid samples: 3  # Load with custom column names df2 <- data.frame(   sample_id = c("A", "B"),   y_coord = c(43.42, 43.43),   x_coord = c(-73.69, -73.68),   reservoir = c("Lake One", "Lake One") ) sites <- load_sites(df2, lat_col = "y_coord", lon_col = "x_coord",                     site_col = "sample_id", lake_col = "reservoir") #>   Loaded 2 rows with columns: sample_id, y_coord, x_coord, reservoir #>   Using columns: Latitude = y_coord, Longitude = x_coord #>   Using specified lake name column: reservoir #>   Final valid samples: 2 #>   Detected location from column 'reservoir': Lake One

title: lakefetch 0.1.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-20

title: lakefetch 0.1.3
text: Fixed commented-code @examples add_lake_depth() get_lake_boundary(). Examples now use runnable code throughout.

title: lakefetch 0.1.1
text: Maximum fetch location: fetch_calculate() gains find_max_fetch parameter identifies location lake highest possible fetch. Uses efficient longest-internal-chord algorithm (sub-second per lake) rather brute-force grid search. Returns point location, chord length, bearing, optionally full directional fetch profile. Relative exposure classification: Sites now classified absolute fetch thresholds (exposure_category) lake-relative proportional thresholds (exposure_relative). proportional method classifies sites based ratio effective fetch lake’s maximum possible fetch (longest internal chord), providing lake-size-aware exposure context. Default thresholds: Sheltered < 25%, Exposed > 50%. New output columns: fetch_proportion, lake_max_chord_m, exposure_relative.

title: lakefetch 0.1.1
text: Optimized OSM downloads spread-sites: download_lake_osm() now handles geographically spread datasets (e.g., GLEON’s 429 global sites). site spread exceeds 0.5 degrees, sites grouped spatial clusters (~0.1 degree grid) cluster gets small bounding box query. replaces old single-bbox approach cover entire globe timeout. Tested 50 globally-spread GLEON sites (48/50 matched, 26 min download). Robust Overpass API handling: cluster query retries 3 times across 3 different Overpass servers, 1-second rate limiting queries. Failed clusters reported end users know lake boundaries may missing. Minimum area filter: Water bodies smaller 0.0001 km² (100 m²) automatically filtered download, removing garden ponds, fountains, tiny features. Download progress bar: OSM cluster downloads now display progress bar report elapsed time complete. Custom column names: load_sites() now accepts lat_col, lon_col, site_col, lake_col arguments explicitly specify column names auto-detection doesn’t match data format. Progress bars: Long-running fetch calculations now display progress bars interactive sessions, users can see computation proceeding. Progress shown site buffering, directional fetch calculation, multi-lake sequential processing. Shiny app performance: fetch_app() fetch_app_upload() now use hybrid approach large datasets. small datasets (<=50 sites), rose diagrams pre-rendered popups . large datasets (>50 sites), rose diagrams rays generated demand marker clicked, preventing app freezing crashing startup. Marker clustering: Shiny apps automatically cluster markers 30 sites sites span wide geographic area (>5 degrees), preventing browser slowdowns overlapping markers.

title: lakefetch 0.1.1
text: Invalid (0,0) coordinates: load_sites() now detects removes rows latitude longitude 0, typically indicates missing data rather real location. Geometry processing crash: Fixed st_is_valid() crash processing corrupted empty geometries large OSM downloads. Empty geometries now skipped invalid ones repaired automatically. Buffer match count: Fixed negative match count display assign_sites_to_lakes() st_join produced duplicate rows.

title: lakefetch 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release. ## Features Fetch calculation: Ray-casting algorithm measure directional fetch (open water distance) sampling points shoreline Automatic boundary download: Downloads lake boundaries OpenStreetMap multi-server fallback reliability Multi-lake support: Process multiple lakes single analysis automatic site--lake assignment Name-based matching: Falls back lake name matching spatial intersection fails NHD integration: Optional integration National Hydrography Dataset US lakes (outlets, inlets, watershed area, connectivity classification) Weather integration: Historical weather data Open-Meteo API wave energy calculations Depth estimation: Empirical depth estimation lake surface area Visualization: Static plots (maps, bar charts, rose diagrams) interactive Shiny app Exposure classification: Automatic classification Sheltered/Moderate/Exposed categories

title: lakefetch 0.1.0
text: Analytical validation synthetic lakes known geometry (0% error) Literature validation lakes published morphometry (100% pass rate) Edge case validation islands, complex shorelines, multiple islands (100% pass rate) 446 unit tests via testthat 75% code coverage

title: lakefetch 0.1.0
text: Getting started vignette complete workflow example Validation vignette documenting test methodology results Example datasets: adirondack_sites, wisconsin_lakes, example_lake
